sections.title: Base Entry
sections.body: Enter at +/-1.5 ATR only when the macro regime, GOOSE direction, and live spread signal agree. If only two of three agree, cut the first clip in half.

sections.title: Stress Rule
sections.body: If ATR percentile is above 80, cut size by 50%, widen bands to +/-2 ATR, and require the live z-score to stop worsening before entry.

sections.title: No-Trade Rule
sections.body: Do not buy RTY/ES just because it is statistically cheap if credit spreads are widening, rates are rising, and regional banks are weak.

sections.title: Timing Rule
sections.body: Avoid initiating new layered positions in the first 3-5 minutes after major data releases. Let the synthetic spread print a first reaction, a pullback, and then a second attempt before committing.

sections.title: Confirmation Rule
sections.body: For mean reversion, require price to re-enter from beyond the band. Example: buy only after the spread trades below -1.5 ATR and then recovers back above -1.4 ATR.

sections.title: Layering Rule
sections.body: Use three clips: 40% at trigger, 30% at an additional 0.35 ATR extension, 30% only after stabilization. Never add simply because the trade is losing.

sections.title: Profit Rule
sections.body: Take 1/3 off at the rolling mean, another 1/3 at +0.5 ATR in your favor, and trail the final 1/3 with a 0.5 ATR giveback or a GOOSE regime flip.

sections.title: Invalidation Rule
sections.body: Exit if live z-score extends 0.75 ATR beyond your final layer without reversal, or if macro score flips against the trade by two factors.
